skills.summary: Drive the audit-and-revise flow — ingest → critique → plan → approve → run — for a scientific paper dropped into the ScienceSwarm dashboard. Nine-tool surface locked in plan §1.1.

skills.summary: Fetch bioRxiv or medRxiv preprints by DOI, or search recent preprints

skills.summary: Use when the user asks to organize a project, identify the main research threads, look for duplicate papers, or suggest the next pages or tasks worth creating inside ScienceSwarm.

skills.summary: Fetch papers from PubMed by PMID or DOI, or search PubMed by query

skills.name: Research Radar
skills.description: Every N minutes, scan the scientist's tracked topics, query gbrain for relevant signals, synthesize a personalized briefing, and write it back to gbrain as a Timeline entry on each topic's concept page plus a briefings/ summary page.
skills.summary: Every N minutes, scan the scientist's tracked topics, query gbrain for relevant signals, synthesize a personalized briefing, and write it back to gbrain as a Timeline entry on each topic's concept page plus a briefings/ summary page.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm — Capture & Second Brain
skills.description: Help users capture research notes, papers, and ideas into the Second Brain. Use when someone asks about saving notes, uploading papers, brain setup, wiki pages, or knowledge management.
skills.summary: Help users capture research notes, papers, and ideas into the Second Brain. Use when someone asks about saving notes, uploading papers, brain setup, wiki pages, or knowledge management.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm — Dashboard
skills.description: Help users navigate and use the ScienceSwarm dashboard UI. Use when someone asks about the project workspace, settings, communications panel, or how to configure features in the web interface.
skills.summary: Help users navigate and use the ScienceSwarm dashboard UI. Use when someone asks about the project workspace, settings, communications panel, or how to configure features in the web interface.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm — Getting Started
skills.description: Help users install, configure, and run ScienceSwarm. Use when someone asks how to set up the project, run the dev server, connect services, or troubleshoot first-run issues.
skills.summary: Help users install, configure, and run ScienceSwarm. Use when someone asks how to set up the project, run the dev server, connect services, or troubleshoot first-run issues.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm — Research Radar
skills.description: Help users set up and use the Research Radar feature for personalized daily briefings. Use when someone asks about monitoring papers, getting research updates, configuring radar topics, or running on-demand briefings.
skills.summary: Help users set up and use the Research Radar feature for personalized daily briefings. Use when someone asks about monitoring papers, getting research updates, configuring radar topics, or running on-demand briefings.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm Evidence Triangulation
skills.description: Cross-check a scientific, mathematical, or engineering claim across independent primary sources before acting on it.
skills.summary: A reusable cross-host skill for validating claims against multiple independent sources and surfacing disagreements clearly.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm Library Enrichment
skills.description: Suggest and import legally available missing papers from the local paper graph while preserving gbrain provenance.
skills.summary: Use the local citation graph to suggest missing papers for a research question, request per-session consent before legal downloads, and write downloaded or metadata-only papers to gbrain.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Evidence Grounding
skills.description: Search the project paper library first, then external sources, to collect comparable MD protocols, key papers, structures, targets, ligands, and evidence gaps.
skills.summary: Ground MD planning in project literature before external search and preserve evidence classes for downstream parameter decisions.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Execution Handoff
skills.description: Create a Claude Code or OpenHands-ready execution plan for preparing, running, validating, and collecting artifacts for an MD study without inventing scientific choices.
skills.summary: Bridges scientific planning to local execution by producing a conservative MD Execution Handoff Plan for coding agents.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Parameter Planning
skills.description: Recommend MD parameters with rationale, confidence, assumptions, alternatives, expert-sensitivity labels, and risks if chosen incorrectly.
skills.summary: Creates the Parameter Decision Ledger for force fields, solvent, ions, box, timestep, ensembles, equilibration, runtime, restraints, sampling, and analysis choices.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Protocol Review
skills.description: Review an MD setup before execution and flag blocking assumptions, unsafe defaults, missing controls, reproducibility gaps, and overclaim risks.
skills.summary: Quality gate for the MD pipeline: approve, approve with caveats, or block execution based on scientific and reproducibility risks.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Refinement Planning
skills.description: Decide whether an MD study should stop, rerun, extend, adjust parameters, change system definition, switch methods, seek expert review, or seek experimental validation.
skills.summary: Closes the MD loop by turning results and evidence into a refinement decision with a durable decision update.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Results Interpretation
skills.description: Interpret MD outputs, separate run completion from scientific support, and classify conclusions as supported, suggestive, weak, or unsupported.
skills.summary: Post-run MD interpretation skill that explains metrics, avoids overclaims, and recommends the next step.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD Study Design
skills.description: Clarify a molecular dynamics research goal, decide whether MD is the right method, and create an MD Study Brief with missing scientific inputs and confidence boundaries.
skills.summary: First step of the MD pipeline: turn a biological or therapeutic goal into a simulation question and suitability decision.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm MD System Definition
skills.description: Define the exact molecular system for an MD study, including entities, structures, states, cofactors, protonation, membranes, ions, and preparation risks.
skills.summary: Turns an MD study goal and evidence packet into a precise molecular system definition before parameter planning.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm Paper To Plan
skills.description: Turn a paper, method, or technical writeup into a concrete reproduction or implementation plan with validation checkpoints.
skills.summary: A cross-host planning skill for moving from a source artifact to an executable reproduction or implementation plan.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm Research Reasoning
skills.description: Use optional reasoning prompt patterns for research questions without committing the project to a fixed UI workflow.
skills.summary: Optional reasoning patterns for evidence maps, design critique, next experiments, model-system fit, target prioritization, multimodal interpretation, and decision updates.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM Data Acquisition
skills.description: Pull and curate the country-year (or unit-time) panel needed for synthetic control. Validate balance, coverage, and donor-pool integrity before any modeling.
skills.summary: Second step of the SCM pipeline: assemble a balanced unit-time panel with documented sources and validated coverage.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM From-Scratch Data Authoring
skills.description: Author and review the data acquisition and munging code for a from-scratch synthetic-control panel after the study design is approved.
skills.summary: Second step of the from-scratch SCM workflow: generate only the selected data acquisition and panel-building code, then validate balance and provenance.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM From-Scratch Inference and Placebos
skills.description: Author placebo and sensitivity code for from-scratch SCM fits, including in-space, in-time, and leave-one-out checks.
skills.summary: Fifth step of the from-scratch SCM workflow: generate selected placebo and sensitivity code, then summarize non-parametric inference limits.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM From-Scratch Method Choice
skills.description: Author method-comparison code for from-scratch SCM results and decide whether classic SCM is robust enough to carry forward.
skills.summary: Fourth step of the from-scratch SCM workflow: compare classic SCM with modern variants and gate reporting on method-level robustness.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM From-Scratch Pretreatment Fit
skills.description: Author the classic Abadie synthetic-control fit code for prepared from-scratch panels and gate the result on pre-treatment diagnostics.
skills.summary: Third step of the from-scratch SCM workflow: generate only the classic SCM fit code for approved panels and stop on failed pre-treatment fit.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM From-Scratch Question Design
skills.description: Frame the from-scratch synthetic-control research question and turn it into an approval-gated analysis plan before code exists.
skills.summary: First step of the from-scratch SCM workflow: define the question, estimand, donor pool, assumptions, artifact plan, and approval gate before any R code is generated.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM From-Scratch Results Report
skills.description: Author the final from-scratch SCM reporting code and surface comparable figures, tables, interpretation, and artifact links in the project.
skills.summary: Sixth step of the from-scratch SCM workflow: generate the selected report-rendering code and surface final artifacts with provenance.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM Inference and Placebos
skills.description: Run permutation-based inference for synthetic control: in-space placebos across donors, in-time placebos across alternate treatment years, and leave-one-out donor sensitivity.
skills.summary: Fifth step of the SCM pipeline: produce honest non-parametric inference and falsification tests.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM Method Comparison
skills.description: Run modern SCM variants — generalized SCM (Xu), synthetic DiD (Arkhangelsky et al.), and doubly-robust SC (Ben-Michael et al.) — alongside classic Abadie SCM and gate the result on cross-method sign consistency.
skills.summary: Fourth step of the SCM pipeline: cross-check the classic-SC ATT against modern variants and refuse to report fragile, single-method results.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM Pre-Treatment Fit
skills.description: Fit classic Abadie synthetic control: choose predictors, solve donor weights via the constrained quadratic program, and gate the fit on pre-period RMSPE relative to outcome variability.
skills.summary: Third step of the SCM pipeline: produce the synthetic counterfactual and gate it on pre-period fit quality.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM Question Design
skills.description: Frame an international-relations or social-science research question for synthetic control analysis: identify the treated unit, treatment date, donor pool, outcome of interest, and decide whether SCM is the right method.
skills.summary: First step of the SCM pipeline: turn a substantive question about a policy shock, sanction, treaty, or regime change into an SCM-fit research design.

skills.name: ScienceSwarm SCM Results Rendering
skills.description: Render the synthetic-control results as a single interactive HTML report with animated counterfactual trajectory, donor weights, placebo distribution, what-if falsification chart, method-comparison forest plot, methodology modal, and an auto-generated paper-ready Methods paragraph.
skills.summary: Sixth and final step of the SCM pipeline: produce the publication-grade HTML deliverable.

skills.name: Semantic Scholar Database Wrapper
skills.description: Fetch papers from Semantic Scholar by ID, or search Semantic Scholar by query
skills.summary: Fetch papers from Semantic Scholar by ID, or search Semantic Scholar by query

skills.name: UniProt Database Wrapper
skills.description: Fetch UniProtKB proteins by accession, or search UniProtKB
skills.summary: Fetch UniProtKB proteins by accession, or search UniProtKB
